ledger.result: Sharpe 0.801, 8/8 pairs positive

ledger.result: +0.40R/trade, replicated 2 windows

ledger.name: ICT walk-forward B validates edge
ledger.result: 76.0% vs MC 76.8%, within 1pp

ledger.name: March JPY in isolation 2015-2024
ledger.result: 26% WR, swamped by BOJ/FED

ledger.name: Quarter-end fade at scale
ledger.result: 39% WR, n=366 — small sample was noise

ledger.name: State allocator outperforms v004
ledger.result: Sharpe -0.12 vs v004 0.801

ledger.name: Confirmation protocol outperforms v004
ledger.result: Sharpe -0.17

ledger.name: E1 rate divergence at 60-day hold
ledger.result: FALSIFIED (REST MODE 2026-05-18). 60d hold Sharpe 0.300 vs 20d hold 0.658. Longer hold hurts Sharpe despite higher avg R (0.282→0.355). Win rate collapses (38.8%→26.7%). 20-day is the better period. Side finding: 5-day consistency filter on rate signal shows positive R at 20d — worth testing in state_allocator vs single-day signal.

ledger.name: Calendar edges as v004 size boosts
ledger.result: Negligible effect (+0.0014 Sharpe). Only 30/863 trades hit calendar windows (3.5%). March JPY only pair with signal. Quarter-end barely fires.

ledger.name: ICT walk-forward stability with live paper trades
Stop width invariant to Sharpe
ledger.result: Sharpe invariant to stop width scaling. Tested across 4200 combos — hold period dominates, not stop width.

ledger.name: Library bull regime CRITICAL false positive fixed
ledger.result: GOLDILOCKS_LOW_VOL (severity=-1) top match at 0.9338 caused 0.00x size. Fix: floor 0.50x when primary is bull. Equity trading restored.

ledger.name: Pure carry direction (rate sign) works as standalone
ledger.result: FALSIFIED (REST MODE 2026-05-18). Only GBPUSD (+0.178) and AUDUSD (+0.174) positive Sharpe out of 8 pairs. USDJPY (-0.555) and EURUSD (-5.636) worst. High differential ≠ good carry trade — large adverse moves dominate. Carry works as income overlay inside v004, not as directional signal. Recommended pairs: GBPUSD, AUDUSD only.

ledger.name: March JPY seasonal persists pre-2020 (extended history)
ledger.result: FALSIFIED (REST MODE 2026-05-18). Pre-2020 (n=6): 67% WR but avg R barely negative (-0.058). Looks better than post-2020 (20% WR) but not tradeable — too few occurrences, small edge. 2022 Fed hike cycle (+6% USDJPY in March) confirmed it is permanently broken at scale. No April reversal. Archive this research line.

ledger.name: Library CRITICAL match driven by equity dislocation features
ledger.result: FALSIFIED (REST MODE 2026-05-18). SHALE_SUPPLY_OIL_CRASH primary features are actually bullish equity (above 200SMA, positive momentum) — match is commodity/macro-driven, not equity dislocation. True CRITICAL fingerprint across 26 patterns: consecutive_down_weeks (signal=2.22), sector_dispersion (1.99), realized_vol_90d (1.69). Current market matching on oil-era macro, not equity crash precursors. Bull regime floor fix was correct. Recommend: add get_feature_weights() to AlexandrianLibrary for live decomposition.

ledger.name: Fixed TP/SL outperforms trailing stop on macro-aligned entries
ledger.hypothesis: Time exits (77.3% WR) dramatically outperform trailing_stop exits (17.1% WR). 54% of UNEXPLAINED losses exit via trailing stop on macro-aligned trades. Hypothesis: replacing trailing stop with fixed TP at 3.0-4.0R and fixed SL at -1.0R removes premature exit noise and recovers a portion of the UNEXPLAINED category. Pegasus retrain on 3,983 trades independently found optimal tp_rr = 3.801, consistent with this.
ledger.test_method: Backtest v004 8-pair universe with fixed TP/SL structure. Sweep TP at 2.0R, 2.5R, 3.0R, 3.5R, 4.0R with fixed SL at 1.0R. Compare Sharpe vs current 1.024 trailing-stop baseline.

ledger.name: Per-pair hold optimization v007
ledger.hypothesis: All 5 viable pairs optimized to 5-7 day holds from 60d baseline. Expected Sharpe > 1.10.

ledger.name: NY_PM session is anti-edge (-0.283R avg)
ledger.result: -0.283R avg vs London +0.471R. 102 trade sample.
ledger.action: NY_PM vetoed in pipeline.py stage 5.7. London-only MC pass rate: 90.3%

ledger.name: A+ grade paradox — score anti-correlated with success
ledger.result: A+ WR=13% (-0.375R avg) vs Grade A WR=39% (+0.383R avg). 30 vs 72 trades.
ledger.action: A+ downgraded to A for trade decision in pipeline stage 5.7. Still logged as A+.

ledger.name: pd_alignment component is anti-edge
ledger.result: pd_alignment>0: 20% WR. pd_alignment=0: 35% WR. Delta: -15pp.
ledger.action: QUEUED for testing: zero out pd_alignment weight in ICT scorer.

ledger.name: Forex UNEXPLAINED losses are not resolvable by momentum threshold
ledger.result: Momentum distributions identical between unexplained losses and wins. No clean threshold exists.
ledger.action: UNEXPLAINED category requires a third factor not currently measured.

ledger.name: London-only ICT + Grade A: the exact setup for TP2 hits
ledger.result: London Grade A (n=39): WR=41%, avgR=+0.840, Sharpe proxy=2.093. 11/17 TP2 hits.
ledger.action: This is the system target setup. Pipeline now routes toward this profile.

ledger.name: AUTO: eventual_correct < 1.0000
ledger.result: Separation accuracy 78% on commitment failures vs winners. n=770
ledger.action: CANDIDATE ONLY — do not activate without human review. Test via backtest first.

ledger.name: AUTO: mfe_within_3_bars < 0.3946
ledger.result: Separation accuracy 73% on commitment failures vs winners. n=770
ledger.action: CANDIDATE ONLY — do not activate without human review. Test via backtest first.

ledger.name: Counter-momentum 5d is a size booster (pullback entries have 3× better avgR)
ledger.result: Counter (<-0.2%): 52% WR +0.331R avg | Aligned (>+0.2%): 52% WR +0.107R avg. Same WR, 3× better R. Sizing: counter 1.25×, aligned 0.75×.
ledger.action: Size modifier added to signal_engine._compute_size_multipliers(). Backtester uses median effective risk (per-trade variation requires Numba array support — future work).

ledger.name: VIX slope [0,1) is best forex entry environment
ledger.result: VIX slope [0,1): 75% WR +0.697R. VIX slope [3,5): 62% WR +0.353R. IC=-0.095 p=0.008. Size reducer 0.85× when VIX slope > 3.
ledger.action: VIX slope modifier added to signal_engine._compute_size_multipliers(). Reduces size 0.85× in extreme contango environments.

ledger.name: No single latent feature separates forex COMMITMENT_FAILUREs at IC > 0.15
ledger.result: All 5 candidates tested: commitment_score IC=-0.036, cross_pair_divergence IC=-0.021, time_of_month IC=-0.079, VIX_slope IC=-0.095, momentum_5d IC=-0.032. Max IC = 0.095.
ledger.action: COMMITMENT_FAILUREs appear irreducible from available daily data. Accept ~40% entry failure rate as structural. Sizing modifiers (HYP-031, HYP-032) improve returns without gating.

hypotheses.name: USDJPY regime gate: suppress signals in bull+elevated VIX
hypotheses.result: Removing 50/120 trades (bull+VIX>15) raises USDJPY Sharpe 1.004→1.770. Portfolio 1.2864→1.4396 (+0.153). Largest single improvement since v005.
hypotheses.action: DEPLOYED as v010 in forex_backtester.py USDJPY_VIX_GATE=15.0
hypotheses.mechanism: In bull markets with VIX>15, JPY safe-haven bid fights USD macro signal. Regime mismatch. Filter the cluster, keep the pair.

hypotheses.name: US10Y divergence from EUR/USD as size modifier (not standalone)
hypotheses.result: Raw IC: 0.086 (below 0.15 gate). Conditional divergence signal (10yr rises >20bps, EUR flat): 70% hit rate n=125, avg -0.921% 15d forward. p<0.0001. Not promotable as standalone — test as size_mult boost layered on existing macro signal.
hypotheses.action: QUEUED — backtest as EUR/USD size_mult=1.25x when divergence event AND macro already aligned
hypotheses.mechanism: US10Y leads EUR/USD by 5-15 days. When they diverge (rate moved, spot hasnt reacted), reversion is high-conviction.

hypotheses.name: market_structure score is an anti-edge — mirrors HYP-024
hypotheses.result: IC=-0.146 overall. London ms=0: WR=50%, avgR=+1.286 (n=28). London ms>0: WR=22%, avgR=+0.069 (n=73). London UTC03 Grade A ms=0: WR=100%, avgR=+2.875 (n=6). London UTC03 Grade A ms>0: WR=25%, avgR=-0.031 (n=8). A+ London ms>0: WR=17%, avgR=-0.179 (n=36). ROOT CAUSE: BOS/CHOCH confirmation (ms score) = lagging indicator. Trade enters AFTER displacement is done. Same mechanism as pd_alignment (HYP-024).
hypotheses.proposed_action: Set market_structure weight=0.0 in config/ict_params.yml. Same one-line change as HYP-024.
hypotheses.mechanism: ICT scoring rewards confirmation signals that are structurally late. ms=0 trades enter during displacement before structure confirms — that is where the edge lives. ms>0 trades confirm structure has shifted, meaning the move is already committed.
hypotheses.deploy_note: market_structure weight → 0.0. sweep→4.0, fvg_tap→3.0, displacement→1.0. Wired in config/ict_params.yml + ict/pipeline.py 2026-05-23

hypotheses.name: XGBoost vs Neural Net R-multiple prediction
hypotheses.hypothesis: H0: XGBoost and 3-layer ReLU perform equally on R-multiple prediction
hypotheses.conclusion: H0 NOT rejected. NN wins by only 0.006 correlation (<<0.05 threshold). XGBoost wins on Sharpe. NN wins on calibration. Keep XGBoost — simpler, better Sharpe, trivial correlation difference.
hypotheses.action: KEEP_XGBOOST — no change to production model

hypotheses.name: Three-confirmation gate (term structure + options skew) for commodity-linked pairs
hypotheses.hypothesis: For AUDUSD and USDCAD: require WTI/copper term structure confirmation + options skew fear zone before entry. All three must align: rate differential signal + term structure + skew.

gates.USDCAD: WTI slope_20 vs slope_60 (curve shape) + OVX in [15,40] fear zone
gates.AUDUSD: Copper slope_20 vs slope_60 + VIX in [12,30] fear zone

AUDUSD.verdict: HURT — copper gate redundant with existing VIX regime gate

USDCAD.verdict: CONFIRMED — term structure removes ~71% of signals, WR lifts from 41.8% to 51.2%

hypotheses.conclusion: Term structure gate is a genuine edge for USDCAD: Sharpe +0.375, WR +9.4pp. AUDUSD already has VIX regime gate — copper filter is redundant and hurts. Apply USDCAD gate only.
hypotheses.action: Wire WTI term structure gate for USDCAD only. Test USDCAD re-entry into portfolio at gated Sharpe 0.701. Do NOT apply copper gate to AUDUSD.
hypotheses.next_hypothesis: HYP-031: USDCAD portfolio re-entry — does adding USDCAD at 0.701 Sharpe improve portfolio Sharpe from 1.72 via diversification benefit?

hypotheses.name: USDCAD portfolio re-entry with term structure gate
hypotheses.hypothesis: Adding USDCAD at gated Sharpe 0.701 to 5-pair v012 portfolio improves portfolio Sharpe.
hypotheses.conclusion: Diversification win: USDCAD hurts the simple avg Sharpe metric (-0.169) but improves true portfolio Sharpe (+0.015) via decorrelation. CAD/oil is uncorrelated enough with the EUR/GBP/JPY rate pairs that it adds genuine diversification. The improvement is small (0.015) — not enough to justify complexity yet.
hypotheses.action: HOLD: do not add USDCAD in v013. The diversification win (+0.015 true Sharpe) is real but marginal. Revisit if USDCAD gated Sharpe improves above 1.0 with additional signal refinement.
hypotheses.note: Key insight: true portfolio Sharpe (2.76) is significantly higher than avg individual Sharpe (1.72) — confirming the pairs are meaningfully decorrelated. This is already institutional-grade diversification.

hypotheses.name: Stochastic Drift Decomposition — SNR entry gate
hypotheses.hypothesis: H0: Separating drift from Brownian noise (SNR = |μ|/σ) produces no improvement in directional prediction accuracy vs baseline. H1: When SNR > 1.5, directional accuracy improves by > 10pp.
hypotheses.method: Fit SDE dX=μdt+σdW. Estimate μ=20d rolling mean, σ=20d rolling std, shifted by 1 day (no lookahead). SNR=|μ|/σ daily. Split trades: HIGH_SNR>1.5 vs LOW_SNR≤1.5. Compare win rate, avg R, Sharpe. Two-sample t-test on R-multiples. Holdout: 2024 only. Script: scripts/hyp_032_snr_gate.py
hypotheses.accept_if: p<0.05 AND delta_R>0.15 AND delta_WR>10pp AND holdout replicates
hypotheses.practical_implication: Add SNR>1.5 entry gate. Only fire trades in high drift-to-noise regime.
hypotheses.connection: Layer 1 of 3-stack with HYP-033 and HYP-036. Tells WHEN to trade.
hypotheses.conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED. SNR p75 threshold=0.2531. Train: delta_R=-0.155, delta_WR=-0.7pp, p=0.1469. HIGH_SNR regime (top quartile of |μ|/σ) slightly underperforms LOW_SNR on daily forex data. Drift signal on 20-day rolling window is insufficient to constrain direction on daily returns — noise dominates at this timescale. The SDE decomposition is valid theory but wrong application layer for daily holds.
hypotheses.action: CLOSE. Do not add SNR gate. Consider testing on weekly returns or longer hold periods where drift accumulates relative to noise.

hypotheses.name: Monte Carlo State Interference — path convergence size modulator
hypotheses.hypothesis: H0: MC path convergence score at entry has no relationship to subsequent trade outcome. H1: When >65% of 1000 simulated forward paths agree on direction within hold period, WR exceeds baseline by >8pp.
hypotheses.method: At each entry, simulate 1000 GBM paths using prior-window μ and σ (lagged 1d, no lookahead). convergence_score = max(long_fraction, short_fraction). HIGH_CONV: score>0.65. Spearman rho between convergence and R-multiple. Bin analysis: [0.50-0.55][0.55-0.60][0.60-0.65][0.65-0.70][0.70-0.80][0.80+]. Holdout: 2024. Script: scripts/hyp_033_mc_convergence.py
hypotheses.accept_if: Spearman rho>0.15 AND p<0.05 AND delta_WR>8pp at HIGH_CONV AND holdout replicates
hypotheses.connection: Layer 2 of 3-stack with HYP-032 and HYP-036. Tells HOW MUCH CONFIDENCE to have.
hypotheses.conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED. GBM path convergence (N=1000) shows no positive relationship to trade outcome. Train: rho=-0.018 p=0.0608. Holdout: rho=-0.026 p=0.0045. Convergence score is structurally capped near 0.65-0.70 (GBM with estimated daily μ/σ rarely produces >70% path agreement). The 0.65 HIGH_CONV threshold selected almost no trades (-48.9pp vs baseline). The concept depends on HYP-032 being true — if SNR doesn't predict direction, convergence score inherits that failure.
hypotheses.action: CLOSE. Path convergence from pure GBM on daily returns has no edge. Would require regime-conditional μ to be meaningful.

hypotheses.name: Random Matrix Theory Noise Filtering — RMT covariance cleaning
hypotheses.hypothesis: H0: RMT-cleaned covariance matrix produces no improvement in risk-adjusted returns vs raw covariance. H1: RMT eigenvalue cleaning reduces max drawdown by >15% while maintaining Sharpe.
hypotheses.method: Build 252-day rolling correlation matrix for 5 pairs. Apply Marchenko-Pastur filter (q=T/N=252/5=50.4, λ_max=(1+√(1/q))²≈1.302). Eigendecompose, clean noise eigenvalues below λ_max, reconstruct. Run parallel backtests: Portfolio A uses raw C for sizing, Portfolio B uses C_clean. Same signals and entries, different sizing only. Paired t-test on monthly returns. Holdout: 2024.
hypotheses.accept_if: Drawdown reduction > 15% AND Sharpe maintained AND correlation matrix more stable in stress
hypotheses.practical_implication: Replace covariance input in kelly_engine.py with RMT-cleaned matrix recomputed monthly.
hypotheses.connection: Layer 3 of 3-stack. Independent of HYP-032/033 — can deploy standalone. Tells HOW TO SIZE correctly.
hypotheses.conclusion: H0 NOT REJECTED for performance metrics. RMT cleaning produces no material Sharpe or drawdown improvement via per-trade correlation penalty sizing. However: (1) 30.2% of correlation matrix variance is confirmed as MP noise, (2) COVID stress test shows +13.7% correlation matrix stability gain — real signal for risk management during drawdowns. The hypothesis was testing the wrong application layer. RMT benefit is NOT in per-trade size adjustments (too granular) — it is in portfolio-level Kelly fraction optimization and drawdown management.
hypotheses.action: REFRAME: Do not use RMT for per-trade sizing. Use for monthly Kelly fraction optimization — replace portfolio covariance input in risk engine with RMT-cleaned matrix. Build HYP-036: test RMT at portfolio allocation level (monthly rebalance) vs drawdown-period risk reduction.
hypotheses.key_finding: Signal eigenvalue structure is real: 2 of 5 eigenvalues survive MP filter. 30% of apparent correlation is noise. The application layer was wrong in this test, not the theory.

hypotheses.name: RMT Covariance at Portfolio Kelly Level — monthly rebalance
hypotheses.hypothesis: H0: RMT-cleaned covariance at monthly portfolio Kelly rebalance produces no improvement vs raw covariance. H1: RMT cleaning reduces drawdown >15% during stress periods while maintaining Sharpe.
hypotheses.method: HYP-035 tested per-trade correlation penalty — wrong application layer. This tests portfolio-level: monthly rebalance of Kelly fractions using RMT-cleaned C vs raw C. Track cumulative drawdown during stress windows (COVID, 2022 rate shock). Script: scripts/hyp_036_rmt_portfolio_kelly.py
hypotheses.accept_if: Drawdown materially lower during stress AND Sharpe maintained AND 2024 holdout replicates
hypotheses.practical_implication: Replace portfolio covariance input in kelly_engine.py with RMT-cleaned matrix recomputed monthly.
hypotheses.connection: Layer 3 of 3-stack. Reframe of HYP-035 (PARTIAL_CONFIRMED) at correct application layer. Tells HOW TO SIZE correctly.
